content.full_text:
LAW OFFICES OF

NIELSEN, MERKSAMER,
HODGSON, PARRINELLO & MUELLER

SACRAMENTO

770 L STREET, SUITE 800
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
TELEPHONE (916) 446-6752

A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

591 REDWOOD HIGHWAY, 94000

MILL VALLEY, CALIFORNIA 94941

TELEPHONE (415) 389-6800

SAN FRANCISCO

650 CALIFORNIA STREET, SUITE 2650
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94108
TELEPHONE (415) 989-6800

October 22, 1990

Jill Stecher, Esq.
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Re: Sally Anne Sheridan
Mayor of the City of Irvine
P.O. Box 19575
Irvine, CA 92713-9575

Dear Ms. Stecher:

This letter requests formal written advice pursuant to
Government Code section 83114(b) and 2 California Code of
Regulations section 18329(a). Ms. Sheridan has authorized me to
make this request on her behalf. The letter contains all facts
material to the consideration of the questions presented below.

We spoke on the telephone on October 4, 1990, regarding the
conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act
("Act") as they relate to factual scenario described below.
Though we spoke only in general terms, you advised me at that
time that the employer of an employee who is a source of income
to a public official is not also a source of the official's
income. This letter also requests written confirmation of that
oral advice.

Facts

Mayor Sheridan is a real estate agent in Newport Beach. She
often represents individuals in real estate transactions who are
employees of companies with matters pending before the Irvine
City Council. The commissions she receives on these transactions
normally exceed $250.

Mayor Sheridan's husband is also a real estate agent in
Newport Beach. He also represents individuals who are employees
of companies with matters pending before the Irvine City Council.
His commissions normally exceed $500.

Jill Stecher, Esq.
October 22, 1990
Page 2

     The employees/clients do not hold ownership interests in
their companies.
                              Questions

     May Mayor Sheridan participate in decisions of the City
Council which have a reasonably foreseeable, material financial
effect on companies whose employees she has represented in real
estate transactions?

     May Mayor Sheridan participate in decisions of the City
Council which have a reasonably foreseeable, material financial
effect on companies whose employees her husband has represented
in real estate transactions?

                              Analysis

     2 California Code of Regulations section 18704.3 addresses
disqualification relative to income earned as a real estate
agent. If the commission earned by the agent is over $250, the
regulation states that the agent must disqualify him or herself
from participating in any decision which would affect: (1) the
brokerage business where the agent works; (2) the person the
agent represented in the transaction; and (3) any person to which
the agent paid a referral fee.

     Applying this regulation to the facts at issue, Mayor
Sheridan must disqualify herself from decisions affecting her
clients, the brokerage business where she works, and anyone to
which she has paid a referral fee. The same conclusion applies
to clients of her husband. The Act does not require her,
however, to disqualify herself from participating in decisions
affecting the employers of her, or her husband's, clients. This
conclusion applies as long as neither Mayor Sheridan nor her
husband hold any other financial interests in the companies
coming before the City Council.

                              Conclusion

     As mentioned above, Mayor Sheridan and her husband have
several clients plus several prospective clients, who are
employed by companies with matters before the City Council. She
will thus face this situation regularly in the near future and
need confirmation of her obligations under the Political Reform
Act.

Jill Stecher, Esq.
October 22, 1990
Page 3

     We appreciate your time and consideration.  Please call me
if you need any further information.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        James R. Sutton

JRS/sfr

6560.01

LAW OFFICES OF

NIELSEN, MERKSAMER,
HODGSON, PARRINELLO & MUELLER
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

SACRAMENTO

591 REDWOOD HIGHWAY, #4000

MILL VALLEY, CALIFORNIA 94941

770 L STREET, SUITE 800
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
TELEPHONE (916) 446-6752

TELEPHONE (415) 389-6800

650 CALIFORNIA STREET, SUITE 2650
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94108
TELEPHONE (415) 989-6800

November 12, 1990


Jill Stecher, Esq.
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Re: Letter No. 90-672

Dear Ms. Stecher:

    We recently requested advice from the FPPC on the duties of Sally Anne Sheridan, the Mayor of Irvine, under the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act. The letter was written by me, on behalf of Mayor Sheridan; we therefore assume that the FPPC will address its response to me.

    As I mentioned to you on the telephone last week, we would like to change the requestor of the advice. Our firm provides legal advice on political law issues to the Irvine City Attorney, rather than representing Mayor Sheridan directly. The City Attorney has decided that all FPPC Advice Letters relating to Irvine city officials should be addressed to him.

    You indicated that the FPPC could change the name of the requestor as long as you received authorization from the "new" and "old" requestors. We would appreciate the FPPC addressing its advice letter to John Fellows, Irvine City Attorney. His authorization is being sent to you separately.

    Thank you very much for your cooperation.


                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        James R. Sutton

JRS/df
